name: Hawaiian Grilled Fish Salad with Pineapple
recipeIngredient: 6 firm fish steaks or fillets (salmon, halibut, swordfish; tuna, mahimahi, amberjack; or opah), about 6 ounces each
2 cans crushed pineapple in juice (8 ounce)
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse fish in cool water and pat dry.
2. Combine 2 teaspoons oil, 2 tablespoons lemon juice, and 1 teaspoon ginger. Coat both sides of fish. Refrigerate for up to an hour.
3. To prepare pineapple salsa, heat remaining oil in a medium skillet. Add yellow pepper and onion and sauté until soft (about 4 minutes).
4. Add crushed pineapple, raisins, cayenne, and remaining lemon juice and ginger; sauté until pineapple is warm (about 4 minutes longer). Salsa can be made ahead up to 24 hours but bring to room temperature before using.
5. Grill fish 4 to 5 inches from hot coals, turning once, until barely opaque in the center and just begins to flake when tested with a fork (about 2 minutes per side for 3/4-inch thick fish steaks).
6. Divide spinach and romaine among six plates and place a grilled fish steak on each. Top with pineapple salsa as a dressing.
7. Yield: 6 servings.
